briefs.title: BC Consumer Protection Rules Tighten: New Contract and Dispute Rights Take Effect August 2026
briefs.summary: Starting August 1, 2026, BC businesses must follow updated consumer contract rules, including a $50 threshold that triggers written contract requirements for ongoing purchases, and a $5,000 limit protecting customers' right to take small disputes to court instead of private arbitration.
briefs.action_required: Review your customer contracts and dispute resolution clauses to ensure they comply with the new BC rules by August 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: BC Consumer Protection Amendment Act 2025 — updated contract and dispute rules take effect
